textPlain: Hmm, scrolling lags even without javascript (Firefox ESR, Linux). Last time I saw this I think they fix was something about gradients/blur?There's also some kind of weird input-capture stopping keyboard scrolling at first, and the video player is some weird thing I can't see how to make work.
Fantastic read and a really interesting company I did not know about until just now.I would love to see how it handles Castlevania II.
It would be neat if a fuzzer could help set a new tool-assisted speedrun (TAS) record.

Would expect some route optimization, there's spots where it bomb hops around corridor before proceeding. Seems like it could see running straight through would result in same game state soonerBut I'm probably viewing this from TAS perspective instead of fuzzer perspective
For some reason, the embedded videos seem to break in Firefox Private Browsing (128esr). This had me stumped for a while until I tried it in a normal not-private window and it worked.
What’s antithesis? Consider that every blog article you write may be the reader’s first exposure to your company/project.
Haven’t tried Castlevania II, but here’s the first one: https://antithesis.com/blog/castlevania/
> I would love to see how it handles Castlevania II.I assume you're thinking specifically of using the red crystal to spawn a tornado: https://youtu.be/Mx9PwRIK9Io
Yeah the company sounds interesting. I wish the main page had clearer info about what it does. There’s a lot of text but I want the simple, “here’s the little bit of example code to get going.”

Yes, this is a really fun idea and something that we want to do. Though these days we’re setting our sights higher than Nintendo…A funny story though: a regular conference gimmick we have is “Man vs. Machine” where we have attendees race our fuzzer to the end of Mario level 1-1. We did this at the final year of Strange Loop, and the fuzzer was winning handily until not one, not two, but three different professional speedrunners walked by and destroyed us.
The longer you run it, the cleaner the run gets. But Metroid is a very compute-intensive game to fuzz, and we were already nearing the limits of what BigQuery could do for us with that run.
Curious - what OS? (I work at Wistia!)
I thought the same thing. They are quite verbose in explaining themselves but I found their docs to be useful.https://antithesis.com/docs/
